item.title: Language, power and the fear of borrowed words
item.description: In recent months, a curious linguistic anxiety has gripped Bangladesh. Words like ‘azadi’, ‘inquilab’, ‘insaf’, ‘bayan’, ‘bandobast’, ‘majlum’ and ‘faisala’ have migrated from slogans and speeches into television talk shows and Facebook posts.
